name: Capital Punishment Chili
recipeIngredient: 4 pounds Extra lean chuck, chili grind
2 pounds Extra lean pork, chili grind
1 pound Extra lean chuck, cut into 1/4-inch cubes
1/2 cup Wesson oil or kidney suet
1 teaspoon Mole (powdered), also called mole poblano
1 teaspoon Coriander seed (from Chinese parsley or cilantro)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, combine paprika, oregano, MSG, chili powder, cumin, beef bouillon, beer, and 2 cups water. Let simmer.
2. In a separate skillet, brown the meat in batches using 1 pound or 1 1/2 pounds of Wesson oil or suet. Drain and add to the simmering spices. Continue cooking until all the meat is done.
3. Sauté chopped onion and garlic in 1 tablespoon of oil or suet. Add to the spice and meat mixture. Add water as needed. Simmer for 2 hours.
4. Stir in mole, sugar, coriander seed, hot sauce, and tomato sauce. Continue simmering for 45 minutes.
5. Dissolve masa harina flour in warm water to form a paste. Add it to the chili. Season with salt to taste. Let simmer for an additional 30 minutes.
6. For a spicier flavor, add more Louisiana Red Hot Sauce as desired.
WARNING: Ensure food safety by not overheating or overcooking any ingredients.
